description_long: This object is a rectangular, portable radio receiver with a prominent telescopic antenna extending from the top. The front face features two large circular speaker grilles on either side, flanking a central rectangular panel that likely serves as a display or tuning scale. Above the central panel are several tactile buttons and two round dials, presumably for volume and tuning adjustments. The casing appears to be made of molded plastic with a matte finish, and the speaker grilles are metallic. The back is mostly plain, with a vented section for heat dissipation and a handle integrated into the top for carrying. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of classic boombox-style radios found in urban environments.
description: A rectangular, portable radio receiver with a telescopic antenna, dual front speaker grilles, and tactile dials and buttons on top. The casing is matte plastic with metallic speaker covers and a central display panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large circular speaker grilles, a central display panel, and multiple buttons and dials on the top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the radio, the protruding speaker grille, and the extended antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain back with a vented section and the handle, as well as the antenna extending diagonally.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the radio's thickness and the side profile of the speaker grille and antenna.
